patch.value: GH-191: test --add command parser should accept quoted commands containing --
Mirror GitHub issue #191. Improve parser handling and diagnostics for --add command values that contain nested '--' tokens so agent-generated commands are accepted reliably.

value.author: codex-active-pm-cycle-20260610
value.text: Source: GitHub issue https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/191. Duplicate-check run (pm search test --add command parsing terms) found no open canonical item for this scope, so this issue captures that lineage.

value.author: codex-impl
value.text: Post-merge note: PR #198 added robust --add-json linked-test input, parser docs, and regression coverage for complex commands, which resolved GH-193. This item remains open because GH-191 specifically asks for the two-token form pm test <id> --add command "... -- ..." to be accepted or produce targeted syntax guidance; that exact UX remains intentionally unresolved.

value.author: test-parser-agent
value.text: REPRODUCTION (worktree agent-a32c4c78fe4ffcb40, isolated temp project): BEFORE the fix, the two-token form 'pm test <id> --add command "npm test -- parser"' failed with commander 'too many arguments for test. Expected 1 argument but got 2' and only generic invalid_command_usage guidance. Root cause: Commander binds the bare key token ('command') as the --add option value, so the quoted command string becomes an excess positional. The quoted value itself was never the problem - it is one shell token; the parser had no concept of the key-then-value two-token shape.

value.author: test-parser-agent
value.text: DESIGN DECISION (accept AND guide): (1) ACCEPT: new bootstrap normalization mergeLinkedTestTwoTokenEntries (src/cli/bootstrap-args.ts) rewrites 'pm test <id> --add command "... -- ..."' to the documented '--add command=...' form before Commander parses. Merge fires only when commandName=test, the flag is --add/--remove, the next token is an entry-identity key (command/cmd/path for --add; command/path for --remove), and EXACTLY ONE non-flag token follows (the quoted-value shape) - so it is unambiguous and never swallows the item id. Traced as bare_key_value/high in the bootstrap trace. (2) TARGETED GUIDANCE: unquoted multi-token values remain ambiguous, so error-guidance now classifies 'too many arguments' on pm test with --add/--add-json/--remove as linked_test_value_not_quoted, listing all accepted forms (key=value, two-token quoted, --add-json) and synthesizing a copy-pasteable suggested_retry that re-joins the shell-split tokens (e.g. pm test pm-x --add "command=npm test -- parser"). Never-block: agents always get a working next command.

value.author: test-parser-agent
value.text: VALIDATION: pnpm build OK; pnpm typecheck OK; full fast suite via node scripts/run-tests.mjs test: 149 files / 2682 tests passed (touched specs: bootstrap-args.spec.ts + error-guidance.spec.ts = 99 tests; test-command/linked-test-parsers/linked-command-detection/shared-constants-errors = 93 tests). Manual AFTER evidence in temp project: (a) pm test <id> --add command "node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- parser" now ADDS the linked test (ok:true); (b) GH-191 exact repro --add command "npm test -- parser" now parses and correctly reaches the intended sandbox-safety POLICY error instead of a syntax error; (c) two-token --remove command "..." and --add cmd/path variants work, incl. with trailing flags like --run; (d) unquoted form emits linked_test_value_not_quoted guidance with suggested_retry; (e) other commands keep generic excess-args guidance. New error code registered in TELEMETRY_ERROR_CATEGORY_BY_CODE as usage. Docs: COMMANDS.md linked-tests section documents the two-token form. Item left OPEN for lead closeout.

value.note: mergeLinkedTestTwoTokenEntries: accepts two-token --add/--remove key value form pre-Commander
linked_test_value_not_quoted targeted guidance + buildLinkedTestQuotedRetryCommand retry synthesis
two-token merge branch coverage + e2e normalizeBootstrapInvocation cases
